previousHardFailures: chart highlights a different subset (top 10 by volume, e.g. Austria/Georgia at 0.3%) than the top-10-by-probability the prompt requested, contradicting the response table
promptSwap.rationale: Original prompt asked for 'top 10 by current implied probability and chart each country's YES price as a horizontal bar' but the model used get_top_markets sortBy=volume, producing Austria/Georgia (0.3% probability, high volume) on the chart while the table correctly showed Finland/Greece (top by probability). Tightened version (a) names the sort field explicitly ('YES price descending'), (b) anchors with named leaders Finland 34.6% and Greece 17.8% so the model has visual entity targets, (c) enforces table-and-chart-must-match in the same descending order, and (d) adds an explicit 'do not substitute a top-10-by-volume list' guardrail. answerabilityNote also pins the recommended tool call (get_event_outcomes sortBy='price') and explicitly forbids the get_top_markets sortBy='volume' path that previously caused the substitution.
promptSwap.rewrittenPrompt: Inside the Polymarket 'Eurovision Winner 2026' event (50 countries, May 16 final, Finland leading at ~34.6% YES, Greece around 17.8%), rank the top 10 countries strictly by current YES price descending — highest implied probability first — and chart THAT EXACT same 10-country ranking as a horizontal bar of YES price per country. The chart bars must contain the same 10 countries shown in the response table in the same descending YES-price order; do not substitute a top-10-by-volume list and do not chart any metric other than YES price.

deferredRootCauseCategories.reason: One ablation per iteration (SKILL rule). Prompt 10 also fits prompt_underspecified but its underlying failure mode (synthesis ignored explicit 'Skip the resolved April 22/24/30 deadlines' directive in the prompt) is arguably a synthesis-safety runtime issue, not a prompt issue — defer until the synthesis-safety patches in chart-runtime-handoff.json land. Re-evaluate after /query-runtime-research-loop pass.

Reviewer score 4.67 — the response substance already passes the rubric. The validator's 'sparse output' regex is hitting a truncated text preview, not the actual response. Fix the validator signal in context/lib/showcase/* rather than swapping the prompt. Not prompt-fixable here.

Chart artifact has whaleValueUsd: null and whaleCount: null even though the response narrative table contains the dollar figures, so the runtime emitted a chart with the requested metric series literally null. This is a contributor-data → chart-payload plumbing bug, not prompt-fixable. Defer to /mcp-contributor-review-deploy or to the runtime track (synthesis-safety should refuse to emit a chart whose requested series is all-null).

Asks for 30-day evolution but contributor's price-history endpoint returned only the last 24 hours for this market. Could swap to a 24-hour intraday tracker, but the same time-series x-axis runtime defect (chart_time_series_no_date_axis) would still hit it on the next pass. Defer prompt-side rewrite until the chart_time_series_no_date_axis runtime fix lands.

weakQueriesByEvaluationSet.note: Holdout vs train split is informational only for showcase-mode iterations — showcase has a single buyer-pass-fail rubric run end-to-end, not a train/holdout regression check. Listed here per the schema; the swap targets a train-set prompt (05) and the runtime handoff covers both sets.

recommendation: Run /query-runtime-research-loop with VALIDATE_AGAINST=local on the listed fix targets in chart-runtime-handoff.json BEFORE re-running /run-showcase. The prompt 05 swap alone will not unblock the showcase: 8+ runtime defects remain that reproduce on any prompt of the same shape (numeric-categorical date-axis on prompts 09/12, double percentage scaling on prompt 09, raw-id xKey on prompts 06/11, duplicate artifacts on prompts 08/14/16, missing-chart-emission on prompts 17/18/19, time-series-no-date-axis on prompts 01/03/04, categorical-as-line on prompts 11/15/16, series-metric-mismatch on prompts 11/13/14/16, row-count-truncation on prompts 01/04/11). Also fix the SKILL Phase 3b capture-pipeline regression so the 5 viewport_only_screenshot prompts (06, 09, 12, 13, 14) can be re-evaluated with full-page evidence in the next pass.
